risk_notes: Hardcoded secret in config: app.py sets app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = 'your-secret-key-here'. Replace with an environment-sourced secret before deployment.
Application runs with debug enabled: app.run(debug=True, port=5000) in app.py. Debug mode should not be used in production.

Remote CDN dependency: Bootstrap CSS and JS are loaded from jsDelivr in templates/base.html. No Subresource Integrity (SRI) attributes are present, so browser-side dependency integrity is not pinned to a cryptographic hash.
SQLite dependency is referenced but not version-pinned; the effective version comes from the local Python/runtime environment.
The instructions use pip install -r requirements.txt, which installs from a pinned requirements file; no hash pinning is present, so packages are version-pinned but not artifact-pinned.

No verified CVE IDs found in the provided web results for the pinned versions. Snyk reports no known direct vulnerabilities for latest Flask and no direct vulnerabilities for flask-sqlalchemy latest pages cited: [security.snyk.io](https://security.snyk.io/package/pip/Flask), [security.snyk.io](https://security.snyk.io/package/pip/flask-sqlalchemy/3.0.0).

GitHub dependency pages referenced in web results confirm the Flask/Flask-Login/Flask-SQLAlchemy/Werkzeug dependency family in similar Flask tutorial repositories, but they do not provide verified CVE evidence for these exact pinned versions: [github.com](https://github.com/app-generator/docs-flask-simple-app/network/dependencies), [github.com](https://github.com/techwithtim/Flask-Web-App-Tutorial/network/dependencies).
